notes: Specialty boundary is narrow to supported app-to-app integration design, governance, operations, and troubleshooting for packaged enterprise applications.
Authority sources are real public sources from Microsoft, Salesforce, Oracle, and SAP, each with refresh cadence, decay policy, and review date in the manifest.

The package includes specialist-owned scenarios for integration design, mapping and ownership conflicts, monitoring and governance handling, lifecycle constraints, low-confidence escalation, adjacent-specialty rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
The manifest defines bootstrap metadata, delegated request and response contracts, and fallback behavior for shared meta-agents required by issue #146.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness records buyer profile, packaging and subscription framing, proof points, and explicit go-to-market gaps without overstating unsupported integration outcomes.
